titleInfo.title: 關於濟南近郊北魏隋唐造像的補充意見
Guanyu Jinan jinjiao Bei Wei Sui Tang zaoxiang de buchong yijian
Supplementary Views about Sculptures of the Northern Dynasties, Sui and Tang Dynasties in the Suburbs of Jinan

mods.abstract: This is an addendum to Jing Sanlin 1955. He analyses the theory that Huangshi Ya is a result of the evolution of Buddhism from the first center, Langgong gu 郎公谷 （Liubu 柳埠), northward. He thinks this is wrong and he quotes the first dated inscription at Simenta, Shentongsi, of 544, which is later than Huangshi Ya. Liubu was definitely a Buddhist center, but only in the Tang dynasty, when also the sculptures of nearby Qianfo Shan were carved. Jing also mentions the post holes at Huangshi Ya. For Kaiyuan Si, he quotes the first inscription there, dated to the kaihuang era of the Sui, and he says that sutra texts and dharanis were carved there as well. Finally he reports about a giant Buddha head sculpted at Fohui Shan, a donation of the Northern Song..
